rules.message: A tainted-source access (request body/query/params/headers, `searchParams`, `process.argv`) and a dangerous sink (`eval`, `new Function`, `exec`/`spawn`, raw SQL, `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, `.innerHTML`) both appear in the same function â€” this is a coarse v1 check (source+sink co-occurrence, not real dataflow), so confirm the value is actually tainted here. Kept at `warning`, not `critical`: co-occurrence is an approximation of dataflow, not proof of it, and `critical` is reserved for matchers that can prove their claim â€” treat a hit as a lead to confirm, not a confirmed exploit. For a SQL sink, parameterize the query (prepared/parameterized statement API) â€” never escape or interpolate the tainted value into the SQL string. For a shell/process-exec sink, use `execFile`/`spawn` with an args array, not a shell-string `exec()` with string concatenation. For a DOM/HTML sink, use `textContent` (not `innerHTML`) for plain text, or a vetted HTML-sanitizer library if HTML must be inserted â€” never raw `innerHTML` with tainted input. Suppress a vetted case with `// taint-ok`.

`eval(...)` called with a non-literal argument, or `new Function(...)` used at all (even with only literal arguments â€” constructing a function from a string at runtime still defeats CSP's `script-src` and is invisible to every static analyzer downstream, so it is a code smell worth surfacing regardless) â€” building and executing code from a runtime string is one of the highest-risk sinks in JS: if any part of that string can be influenced by user input, it's arbitrary code execution. Avoid dynamic code construction; use a plain function/lookup table/JSON.parse instead. Note: `security/taint-flow` already covers `eval` when a request-derived source co-occurs in the same function, `.ts`/`.tsx` only; this rule is source-free (fires on the construct itself, not just a proven-tainted case) and also covers `.js`/`.jsx`/`.mjs`/`.cjs`. Suppress a vetted case (e.g. a sandboxed plugin-eval with no user-controlled input) with `// eval-dynamic-ok`.
